title: 10 best herbal teas for swollen gums
intro_desc: Herbal teas for swollen gums are natural remedies made from herbs and plants that help reduce inflammation and soothe discomfort in the gums. These teas typically contain anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that help fight off infection and promote healthy gums. Examples of herbal teas that help with swollen gums include chamomile, clove, sage, and calendula. Chamomile tea has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and soothe pain, while clove tea contains eugenol, a natural anesthetic that numbs the gums and reduces inflammation. Sage tea has antibacterial and astringent properties that help reduce inflammation and prevent infection, and calendula tea has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that promote healing. Herbal teas for swollen gums can significantly improve the lives of those suffering from gum disease, as they provide a natural and effective way to manage symptoms and promote oral health.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera tea helps with swollen gums because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce inflammation and pain in the gums. The tea is also rich in antioxidants that help to fight off infection and promote healing. Additionally, aloe vera has antibacterial properties that can help to kill off harmful bacteria in the mouth, reducing the risk of gum disease and other oral health problems. Drinking aloe vera tea can help to soothe and heal swollen gums, promoting overall oral health and wellness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Aloe vera contains compounds such as bradykinase and salicylates that help reduce inflammation, providing relief for swollen gums.
Antibacterial: Aloe vera tea has antibacterial properties due to the presence of anthraquinones and phenolic compounds, which can help prevent gum infections that cause swelling.
Analgesic: Aloe vera contains compounds like magnesium lactate and carboxypeptidase, which have pain-relieving properties, helping to alleviate discomfort from swollen gums.
Wound healing: Aloe vera stimulates fibroblast proliferation and collagen synthesis, promoting tissue regeneration and healing, which can help treat gum injuries causing swelling.

Antioxidant: Aloe vera tea contains various antioxidant compounds, such as flavonoids and ascorbic acid, which help protect gum tissues from oxidative stress and inflammation, supporting overall gum health.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 cup of water and 2 tablespoons of fresh aloe vera gel.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove aloe vera gel pieces.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried chamomile flowers to the tea.
Steep for 5 minutes, then drink or use as a mouthwash for swollen gums.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tea helps with swollen gums because it contains anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can reduce inflammation and fight off infection. The tea is made from the flowers of the pot marigold plant, which are rich in flavonoids and carotenoids that can help to soothe irritated gums and promote healing. Drinking pot marigold tea or using it as a mouthwash can help to alleviate the pain and discomfort associated with swollen gums, and can also help to prevent further inflammation and infection. Additionally, the astringent properties of pot marigold tea can help to tighten gum tissue and reduce bleeding.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tea helps reduce swelling in gums by decreasing inflammation, thanks to its high levels of anti-inflammatory flavonoids and carotenoids.
Antimicrobial: The tea inhibits the growth of harmful bacteria, reducing gum inflammation and preventing infection, due to its active constituents like flavonoids and essential oils.
Analgesic: Pot marigold tea alleviates gum pain by numbing the affected area, owing to the presence of anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving compounds.
Wound healing: The tea promotes gum tissue regeneration and faster healing, as it contains active constituents like flavonoids and carotenoids that stimulate tissue growth.
Astringent: Pot marigold tea tightens gum tissues and reduces gum bleeding, thanks to its tannins that have an astringent effect.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, pour 1 cup of water and bring it to a boil.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried pot marigold flowers to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the flowers.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature before using it as a mouthwash for swollen gums.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Teaberry tea helps with swollen gums because it contains powerful anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce inflammation and pain in the gums. The tea is rich in tannins, which have astringent properties that can help tighten and soothe irritated gum tissue. Additionally, teaberry tea contains antioxidants that can help fight off infection and promote healing. Drinking teaberry tea can also help increase saliva production, which can help wash away bacteria and food particles that can contribute to gum inflammation. Regularly drinking teaberry tea can help maintain good oral health and prevent gum disease.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Teaberry tea contains compounds such as gallic acid and tannins that help reduce inflammation, providing relief for swollen gums.
Antibacterial: The berberine and arbutin in teaberry tea have antibacterial properties, which can help combat gum infections causing swelling.
Analgesic: Teaberry tea's numbing effect, due to its eugenol content, can alleviate pain from swollen gums.
Astringent: The tannins present in teaberry tea act as an astringent, helping to tighten gum tissues and reduce swelling.
Antioxidant: Rich in antioxidants, teaberry tea can help protect gum tissues from further damage and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Crush 2 teaspoons of dried teaberry leaves into a fine powder.
Place the crushed leaves in a tea infuser.
Steep the infuser in a cup of boiling water for 10 minutes.
Remove the infuser and let the tea cool to a comfortable temperature.
Rinse your mouth with the tea, swishing it gently around your gums for 30 seconds.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tea helps with swollen gums because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce inflammation and pain in the gums. The tea is made from the root of the licorice plant, which contains compounds such as glycyrrhizin and flavonoids that have been shown to have antibacterial and soothing effects. These properties can help to fight off the bacteria that cause gum inflammation, reduce redness and swelling, and promote healthy gum tissue. Drinking licorice tea can provide relief from swollen gums and promote oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Licorice tea can reduce swelling in gums by decreasing inflammation, thanks to its active constituents, such as glycyrrhizin and flavonoids.
Antimicrobial: Licorice tea helps control gum inflammation by inhibiting the growth of harmful bacteria, due to the presence of antimicrobial compounds like isoflavonoids and coumarins.
Analgesic: Licorice tea alleviates gum pain associated with swelling, as it contains pain-relieving constituents, such as glycyrrhizin and liquiritigenin.

Glycyrrhizin-induced corticosteroid-like effects: Licorice tea promotes healing in swollen gums by stimulating corticosteroid-like activity, thanks to glycyrrhizin's ability to mimic the functions of natural corticosteroids.
Antioxidant: Licorice tea supports overall gum health by neutralizing free radicals and reducing oxidative stress, due to its rich antioxidant content, including flavonoids and chalcones.
remedy_recipe: In a small pot, add 1 cup of water and bring it to a boil.
Add 1 tablespoon of dried licorice root to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the liquid into a cup, discarding the solids.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before using it as a mouthwash for swollen gums.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Witch hazel tea helps with swollen gums because it contains tannins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce inflammation and pain. The astringent properties of witch hazel may also help to tighten and strengthen gum tissue, reducing the risk of gum disease. Additionally, witch hazel has antibacterial and antiviral properties, which can help to kill harmful bacteria in the mouth that can cause gum inflammation and infection. Drinking witch hazel tea or using it as a mouth rinse can provide relief from swollen gums and promote oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Witch hazel tea's anti-inflammatory property reduces swelling in gums by decreasing inflammation, thanks to its high tannin content.
Astringent: Witch hazel tea's astringent property tightens and strengthens gum tissues, reducing swelling, due to the presence of gallic acid and ellagic acid.

Antioxidant: Witch hazel tea's antioxidant property helps to heal damaged gums and reduce swelling by neutralizing free radicals and promoting tissue repair, attributed to its polyphenol and flavonoid content.
Antibacterial: Witch hazel tea's antibacterial property inhibits the growth of harmful bacteria, minimizing gum inflammation and swelling, due to the presence of gallic acid and tannins.
Analgesic: Witch hazel tea's analgesic property alleviates pain and discomfort from swollen gums by numbing the affected area, owing to its high tannin content.
remedy_recipe: In a stovetop kettle, boil 2 cups of water for 5-7 minutes.
Crush or chop 2 tablespoons of dried witch hazel leaves and bark.
Steep the crushed herbs in boiled water for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea into a glass, discarding the used herbs.
Allow the tea to cool down to a warm temperature before using it as a mouth rinse for swollen gums.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St. John's wort tea helps with swollen gums because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce inflammation and pain in the gums. The tea is made from the flowers, leaves, and stems of the St. John's wort plant, which contains several active compounds, including hypericin and hyperforin. These compounds have been shown to have antibacterial and antiviral properties, which can help to reduce infection and inflammation in the gums. Additionally, St. John's wort tea has been used traditionally as a mouthwash to promote oral health and prevent gum disease. Drinking St. John's wort tea or using it as a mouthwash may help to soothe irritated gums and promote healing.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: St. John's wort tea reduces inflammation, which is crucial for swollen gums as it alleviates irritation and promotes healing. This property is attributed to the active constituents, such as hypericin and hyperforin.

Antimicrobial: The tea inhibits the growth of harmful bacteria, which is important for swollen gums as it prevents infection and further complications. The antimicrobial effects are primarily due to the presence of hyperforin and flavonoids.

Analgesic: St. John's wort tea has pain-relieving properties, which is important for swollen gums as it alleviates discomfort and pain. Hypericin and other constituents contribute to the tea's analgesic effects.

Antioxidant: The tea contains potent antioxidants, which are crucial for swollen gums as they help protect gum tissue from damage and support overall oral health. The antioxidant properties can be attributed to various flavonoids and phenolic acids present in St. John's wort.

Astringent: St. John's wort tea has astringent properties, which are important for swollen gums as they help reduce inflammation and tighten gum tissue. The astringent effects are primarily due to the tea's tannin content.
remedy_recipe: In a cup, combine 1 teaspoon of dried St. John's Wort herb and 8 ounces of boiling water.
Allow the herb to steep in hot water for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the liquid to remove the herb particles.
Let the tea cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Rinse your mouth with the prepared St. John's Wort tea, gently swishing it around the affected areas for 30 seconds to 1 minute. Repeat as needed up to three times a day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tea helps with swollen gums because it contains anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can reduce inflammation and help soothe irritation. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties work to alleviate pain and discomfort, while its antioxidant properties aid in healing and tissue regeneration. Additionally, lavender has natural antimicrobial properties that can help prevent infection and promote oral health. Drinking lavender tea or using it as a mouth rinse can provide relief and promote healthy gums.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: English lavender tea's anti-inflammatory property reduces swelling and irritation in gums, thanks to its active constituents like linalyl acetate and linalool.
Antimicrobial: Its antimicrobial property inhibits the growth of harmful bacteria causing gum inflammation, as the tea contains compounds such as eucalyptol and camphor.
Analgesic: English lavender tea's analgesic property alleviates gum pain, with active constituents like cineole and lavandulol contributing to its pain-relieving effects.
Wound healing: The tea's wound healing property promotes gum tissue regeneration, with active constituents like rosmarinic acid and ursolic acid aiding in tissue repair.

Antioxidant: English lavender tea's antioxidant property combats oxidative stress and inflammation in gums, as its active constituents like apigenin and rosmarinic acid neutralize free radicals and support overall gum health.
remedy_recipe: In a small pot, boil 1 cup of water for 3-5 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried English lavender flowers to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let the mixture simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the lavender flowers.
Allow the tea to cool to a comfortable temperature before using as a mouthwash for swollen gums.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile tea helps with swollen gums because it contains anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can reduce inflammation and fight infection. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe irritated gums, while its antiseptic properties help to kill bacteria that can cause gum disease. Additionally, chamomile tea contains flavonoids, which are plant-based compounds that have been shown to reduce inflammation and promote healing. Drinking chamomile tea or using it as a mouth rinse can help to alleviate the pain and discomfort associated with swollen gums, and promote overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: German chamomile tea contains several anti-inflammatory compounds, including chamazulene and bisabolol, which help reduce swelling in gums by suppressing the body's inflammatory response.

Antibacterial: German chamomile tea has potent antibacterial properties due to its high content of active constituents like azulene and alpha-bisabolol, which help combat gum inflammation by inhibiting the growth of harmful bacteria that cause gum disease.

Analgesic: German chamomile tea contains various analgesic compounds, such as matricin and its derivatives, that help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with swollen gums by numbing the affected area.

Antioxidant: German chamomile tea is rich in antioxidants, including flavonoids and terpenoids, which help protect gum tissue from oxidative stress and damage, thereby reducing inflammation and swelling.

Wound healing: German chamomile tea promotes wound healing due to the presence of active constituents like bisabolol and matricin, which stimulate tissue regeneration and help accelerate the healing process of swollen gums.
remedy_recipe: Measure 1 teaspoon of dried German chamomile flowers.
Place flowers in a tea infuser or teapot.
Pour 8 ounces of boiling water over the chamomile flowers.
Steep for 5-10 minutes, then remove the infuser or strain the tea.
Sip the tea slowly, allowing it to soothe swollen gums.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tea helps with swollen gums because it contains anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that can reduce inflammation and fight off infection. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe and calm irritated gums, while its antiviral properties can help to combat any viral infections that may be contributing to the swelling. Additionally, lemon balm tea has antibacterial properties that can help to kill off harmful bacteria in the mouth that can cause gum inflammation. Drinking lemon balm tea can also help to boost the immune system, which can help the body to fight off any infections that may be causing the swelling.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Lemon balm tea helps reduce swelling in gums by decreasing inflammation, thanks to the rosmarinic acid and eugenol present in the plant.
Antibacterial: Its antibacterial properties, attributed to the citral and geraniol components, inhibit the growth of harmful bacteria causing gum inflammation.
Analgesic: Lemon balm contains tannins that have a pain-relieving effect, alleviating discomfort associated with swollen gums.
Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content, primarily due to rosmarinic acid and various flavonoids, aids in healing and tissue repair in the gums.
Relaxing: Lemon balm has a calming effect, reducing stress and promoting relaxation, which can help manage symptoms of gum discomfort and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, pour 1 cup of water and bring it to a boil.
Add 1 tablespoon of dried lemon balm leaves or 2 tablespoons of fresh leaves to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the tea and let it cool down.
Rinse your mouth with the cooled lemon balm tea or use it as a mouthwash for swollen gums.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oregano tea helps with swollen gums because it contains anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can reduce inflammation and fight off infection. The tea is made from the leaves of the oregano plant, which are rich in essential oils and antioxidants. These compounds can help to soothe irritated gums, reduce pain and promote healing. Additionally, oregano tea has been shown to have antifungal and antiviral properties, making it a powerful tool in maintaining oral health. Drinking oregano tea or using it as a mouthwash can provide relief from swollen gums and improve overall gum health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Oregano tea's anti-inflammatory properties reduce swelling and pain in gums by inhibiting the release of inflammatory chemicals. This is due to the presence of carvacrol and thymol, which have anti-inflammatory effects.

Antibacterial: Oregano tea's antibacterial properties help prevent gum infections that cause swelling by inhibiting the growth of bacteria. The active constituents responsible for this property are thymol, carvacrol, and eugenol.

Antioxidant: Oregano tea's antioxidant properties promote gum health by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the gums. The active constituents responsible for this property are rosmarinic acid and thymol.

Analgesic: Oregano tea's analgesic properties help alleviate gum pain associated with swelling by numbing the affected area. The active constituents responsible for this property are thymol and carvacrol.

Immune-boosting: Oregano tea's immune-boosting properties help the body fight off gum infections that cause swelling by enhancing the body's natural defense mechanisms. The active constituents responsible for this property are thymol and carvacrol.
remedy_recipe: In a small pot, boil 1 cup of water for 3-5 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried oregano leaves to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let the mixture simmer for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the oregano leaves.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before swishing it around your mouth for 30 seconds, then spit it out. Repeat this process 2-3 times daily.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat swollen gums with herbal teas is to use cold chamomile or sage tea bags. Steep the tea bags in hot water for a few minutes, then let them cool down. Place the cooled tea bags directly on the affected area for about 10-15 minutes. Both chamomile and sage have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can help reduce swelling and soothe irritation. Repeat this process several times a day for best results. Additionally, maintain good oral hygiene by brushing and flossing regularly.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to swollen gums that are treated with herbal teas include gingivitis and periodontal disease. Herbal teas like chamomile and clove have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce gum inflammation and pain. Green tea is rich in antioxidants that promote healthy gums and prevent gum diseases. Licorice root tea inhibits the growth of harmful bacteria in the mouth, reducing the risk of gum diseases. Myrrh tea has antiseptic and astringent properties that soothe inflamed gums and promote healing. Regular consumption of these herbal teas can alleviate symptoms of gum ailments and improve oral health.
